rationale: The citing paper references Soman and Cheema (2011) as key prior evidence that earmarking/mental accounting makes funds less fungible and can improve consumer welfare. It then explicitly uses that established earmarking effect to motivate its own Studies 3 and 4, which test whether role integration changes willingness to reallocate specifically earmarked money across roles. Because the citation informs the conceptual framing and the design logic of core studies, it plays a meaningful role beyond a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Soman and Cheema (2011) as a prior foundation for earmarking-induced non-fungibility of funds. It uses that foundation to develop and test a new moderator claim: role integration increases cross-role reallocation of earmarked money.
